methods.description: The method involves calculating an age-depth model using a linear interpolation function between dated levels to reconstruct the chronological sequence from 24 ka BP to the late Holocene.

steps.description: The chronological reconstruction based on the calibrated ages corresponds to time intervals spanning from 24 ka BP to the late Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch calibrated age data for time intervals from 24 ka BP to late Holocene

steps.description: The age-depth model was calculated using a linear interpolation function between dated levels (Figure 3).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate age-depth model using linear interpolation between dated levels

steps.description: Sedimentation rates are variable among the box cores (Figure 3).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sedimentation rates variability among box cores

steps.description: As a general rule, the box cores show the highest sedimentation rates (from 3.62 to 5.99 cm/ky, without considering a temporary drop in BC 04) for the levels approximately older than 15 ka BP, whereas the lowest sedimentation rates (from 0.74 to 1.70 cm/ky) characterise the entire Holocene.
steps.searchable_summary: compare sedimentation rates for levels older than 15 ka BP and Holocene
